The base template, index route, static file serving, and tests were in place. I made two improvements: - Changed skip-link from `.visually-hidden` to `.skip-link` class (already committed by others) - Added `aria-atomic="true"` to the aria-live region in base.html - Added HTMX indicator CSS (`.htmx-indicator` styles) to the components layer - Enhanced the integration test with more assertions for landmarks, CSS link, aria-live region 4. **Commit `9af4522`**: Committed HTMX indicator CSS and aria-atomic improvement. 5. **Task #5 - Registration page**: I created: - `templates/register.html` - Full registration form with email, password, password confirmation - Auth form CSS component (`.auth-form` and sub-components) in `static/css/main.css` - `src/routes/register.rs` - GET handler for rendering the form - `tests/api/register.rs` - Integration test for the form structure - Wired the route in `startup.rs` and `routes/mod.rs` 6. **POST handler for registration**: The team had written tests for POST /register but the handler wasn't wired. I: - Added `post_register` function to `register.rs` with form processing, validation error mapping, and redirect - Exported it from `routes/mod.rs` - Wired it in `startup.rs` - Fixed `Redirect::see_other()` → `Redirect::to()` (Axum 0.8 API difference) 7. **Concurrency issues**: Files kept reverting because other team members were editing the same files simultaneously. This was a recurring problem throughout. 8. **Commit `13e8dc5`**: Large commit including registration form, CSS, route handler, plus other team members' domain files that were on the working tree. 9. **Team-lead directive to STOP and push**: The project owner demanded all code be committed and pushed before any new work. I verified the branch was up to date with origin and committed the remaining `.auth-form__hint` CSS class at `fa39701`, then pushed. 10. **Commit discipline rules proposal**: Team-lead proposed 5 new rules. I gave CONSENT on all 5 rules with detailed justification for each. 11. **CI failure on Task #6 commit**: The login/logout commit (`199694a`) failed CI because `Redirect::see_other()` doesn't exist in Axum 0.8 and tower-sessions version incompatibility. Someone else fixed it (`e2cda97`), CI went green. 12. **Final state**: Branch is up to date with origin. Working tree has uncommitted changes from other team members (Cargo.lock/toml, startup.rs, tests/api/main.rs, tests/api/login.rs). The team-lead sent another STOP directive to commit and push all existing work before writing more code. Key files I need to document: - `/home/jwilger/projects/todo_list/static/css/main.css` - My primary deliverable (design tokens + components) - `/home/jwilger/projects/todo_list/templates/base.html` - Base HTML template - `/home/jwilger/projects/todo_list/templates/register.html` - Registration form - `/home/jwilger/projects/todo_list/templates/login.html` - Login form (created by others) - `/home/jwilger/projects/todo_list/src/routes/register.rs` - Registration route handler - `/home/jwilger/projects/todo_list/src/routes/login.rs` - Login route handler (created by others) - `/home/jwilger/projects/todo_list/src/startup.rs` - Application router with all routes Key errors: - `Redirect::see_other()` doesn't exist in Axum 0.8 - use `Redirect::to()` instead - Files reverting due to concurrent teammate edits - CI failures from tower-sessions version incompatibility - Accidentally including other team members' files in commits - Routes not being exported from mod.rs / not wired in startup.rs Current state: The team-lead's most recent message tells me to STOP writing new code and ensure everything is committed and pushed. Primary Request and Intent: I am operating as **Lea Verou** (CSS/Frontend Engineer) on a 9-person ensemble team building a multi-user todo list app in Rust + HTMX. My role covers CSS architecture, design tokens, HTML templates, and frontend accessibility. The session started from a context compaction recovery where Task #3 (Design tokens) was already completed. No separate `askama_axum` crate needed. - **CSS Cascade Layers**: `@layer reset, tokens, base, layout, components, utilities;` - **HTMX indicator pattern**: `.htmx-indicator` hidden by default (opacity: 0), revealed by `.htmx-request` parent class - **Auth form CSS pattern**: `.auth-form` centered layout with `:has([aria-invalid])` for parent-based error styling - **tower-sessions + PostgresStore**: Session management with `SessionManagerLayer` wired as Axum layer - **Progressive enhancement**: HTML works without JS, HTMX enhances, TypeScript for non-critical extras - **TDD workflow**: Red-Green-Refactor with integration tests against full HTTP stack - **Commit discipline rules** (proposed, consensus reached): (1) Commit every green step, (2) CI gate before new work, (3) Task completion requires CI green, (4) Clean working tree at task boundaries, (5) No red-state abandonment 3.    - `aria-invalid` conditionally set via `.is_some()` check
   - Error paragraphs always rendered with `role="alert"`, content conditionally filled
   - Added password hint "At least 8 characters" with `aria-describedby` linking

` elements with `class="auth-form__error"` -- they're just empty when there's no error. This is the correct accessibility pattern: `aria-describedby` references an element that always exists, and it gets populated with error text when validation fails. The `role="alert"` ensures screen readers announce the error when it appears. The test needs to be updated. Instead of checking that `auth-form__error` class is absent, it should verify that no error *text* is visible on initial load.
